title: GRIMM Experimental Forming Apparatus Documentation
author: Roy, M. J.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
A document which covers a complete overview of how an older manual lathe was retrofitted with the necessary systems to create a machine capable of performing the repeatable rotary forming of cast aluminum blanks, at elevated temperatures, while providing necessary process measurements. The main purpose of this document is to describe each of the subsystems, with a scope limited to design, operation and where applicable, maintenance. This document has been uploaded in support of a recent publication which documents and illustrates the experimental protocol for its use: 

 Roy, M. J., Maijer, D. M. Experimental Procedure for Warm Spinning of Cast Aluminum Components.  J. Vis. Exp.  (120), e55061, doi:10.3791/55061 (2017). 

 It is hoped that this technical paper will assist others in configuring their own low-cost experimental processing equipment.
